title: Utah's Biggest Fire Just Made Weather History
script: Utah's biggest fire ever? That's actually the question, and the answer is yes. The Cottonwood Fire is now the largest wildfire in Utah recorded history, burning past 92,000 acres with zero percent containment. It's also the nation's largest active fire right now. The National Weather Service office in Salt Lake City just issued their very first Particularly Dangerous Situation red flag warning in history, which is the NWS saying conditions are as bad as they have ever measured. A ski resort is damaged and 150,000 acres are burning across the state simultaneously. The governor declared a state of emergency. Crews are fighting a fire that's generating its own wind systems, throwing embers miles ahead. Will anything break the Cottonwood Fire's run?

shots.phrase: biggest fire in Utah recorded history
National Weather Service Salt Lake City issues first Particularly Dangerous Situation NWS warning
